decision: Sign off on route — settlement-first or issue-first — at 9 May client call. ET1 deadline 3 July; £8–12k settlement realistic; partner call on funding given £15k–25k own-side costs to hearing.
rendered:
Decision: settlement-first or issue-first, sign-off needed at 9 May client call.

Top risks
1. Tom Reeves availability. The waiver-in-practice defence to handbook §4.7 turns on him. He has left Acme. If we cannot trace him or he declines, the strongest answer to Acme's best point collapses. Action: trace this week.
2. Polkey reduction. Even on a procedurally strong case the tribunal may find dismissal was substantively inevitable and cut compensatory to a low percentage. Risk: realistic award drops below settlement floor.
3. Documentary chronology on BlueLight. £42k contract signed 18 Feb supports the entertainment account but the dinner pre-dated the contract. Acme may argue the relationship was already commercial and entertainment unnecessary. Need the email trail before WP exchange.

Escalate
- Funding. Compensation ceiling £15,750. Own-side fees to hearing £15–25k on private rates. Net to client thin even on a win. Discuss DBA, CFA, or LEI cover at the 9 May call.
- No insurance notification trigger; small-firm caveat noted.

Settled
- Jurisdiction (ERA 1996, ET, E&W). Continuous service met (3y 2m). ACAS EC complete (Day A 2 May, Day B 24 May). Limitation calculated to 3 July under s.207B.

Partner judgment required on
- Route. Settlement-first preserves Hodgson & Pike's commercial flexibility and keeps costs down; issue-first is a stronger negotiating posture but adds £3–5k irrecoverable on a settled case. View: settlement-first absent reason to think Acme will harden, but defer to partner.
- Approach to Reeves. Informal cooperation first, or witness summons if he declines. Friendly approach risks tipping Acme off.

claims.claim: Tom Reeves's evidence on the waiver-in-practice is decisive on the handbook §4.7 point and he has left Acme
BlueLight contract signed 18 February 2026, two weeks after the dinner
ACAS EC complete (Day A 2 May, Day B 24 May); ET1 deadline 3 July under s.207B
Polkey reduction is a material risk on the compensatory award
Own-side fees to hearing in the £15–25k range on private rates
